_comment: Flow 07 — US multi-airport cross-country KPAO → KSQL with Flight Following. Full howto_us §2 flow: KPAO Ground taxi, Tower departure on course, squawk 1200 on leaving, NORCAL Approach flight following, arrival at KSQL tower (pattern entry from APPROACH_CONTACT), landing, taxi to ramp. Three controllers touched: Ground, Tower, Approach, Tower again. Tests APPROACH_CONTACT → PATTERN_ENTRY transition.
name: US multi-cross-country — KPAO → KSQL (FF)

context.callsign: November One Two Three Alpha Bravo

say.text: Palo Alto Ground November One Two Three Alpha Bravo at the ramp ready to taxi

say.note: KPAO Step 2 — Ground: ready for departure on course
say.text: Palo Alto Ground November One Two Three Alpha Bravo holding short ready for departure on course
Palo Alto Tower November One Two Three Alpha Bravo ready for departure VFR to San Carlos on course

say.note: KPAO Step 4 — Tower: leaving frequency → squawk 1200
say.text: November One Two Three Alpha Bravo leaving your frequency good day

say.note: En-route Step 5 — Approach: request Flight Following
say.text: NORCAL Approach November One Two Three Alpha Bravo two thousand five hundred VFR request flight following

say.note: — Hand off to KSQL airspace. Airport change while EN_ROUTE auto-resets the ATC state machine (atc_session.cpp does this every frame).
KSQL Step 6 — Tower: inbound for landing
say.text: San Carlos Tower November One Two Three Alpha Bravo five miles east inbound for landing
November One Two Three Alpha Bravo midfield left downwind
November One Two Three Alpha Bravo on final full stop
November One Two Three Alpha Bravo clear of the runway

say.note: KSQL Step 10 — Ground: taxi to the ramp
say.text: San Carlos Ground November One Two Three Alpha Bravo request taxi to the ramp
